data.content:
The build generates the file (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` within the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is created on disk, but MSBuild/Roslyn doesn't know to include it in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `ItemGroup` inside the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the `WriteCodeFile` task runs, so the compiler knows to include it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The compiler only processes files listed in the `Compile` item group.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
